Notice Of Motion And Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Cal. Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.98(C) And 1281.99(A)
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Nauman Nadeem's unopposed Motion For Statutory Attorney's Fees and Costs Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sections 1281.98(c) and 1281.99(a) is GRANTED.

The court finds the record supports an award of fees under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1281.98(c) and 1281.99(a).

For purposes of this motion only, the billable hourly rates of Plaintiff's counsel are reasonable and not inconsistent with attorneys of similar experience for similar work in the relevant legal market.

The hours expended by Plaintiffs' counsel are found to be reasonable and limited to those hours that were spent in connection with the abandoned arbitration matter and as a result of Defendants' material breach of the arbitration agreement.

The Court finds the costs expended in the sum of $484.40 to be reasonable, necessary, recoverable, and limited to only those costs that were incurred in connection with the abandoned arbitration matter and as a result of Defendants' material breach of the arbitration agreement.

Accordingly, the Court awards to Plaintiff his attorneys' fees in the total sum of $12,900.00, as well as his costs in the total sum of $484.40, for a total award of attorneys' fees and costs of $13,384.40, payable to JML Law Client Trust Account as payment of Plaintiff's attorneys' fees and costs. Defendants Paradigm Hotels Group LLC and 250 Fourth Development, LP are jointly and severally liable for payment of the $13,384.40 in attorneys' fees and costs to Plaintiff, which shall be paid within 60 days of notice of entry of this Order.

Order Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2024 Set For Trial On Aug-18-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Oct-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PARADIGM HOTELS GROUP, LLC, AND 250 FOURTH DEVELOPMENT, LP's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Defendant failed to timely pay all of the required arbitration costs. (Sarris Decl., pars. 10-11, 15.) Therefore, per CCP 1281.98, plaintiff can litigate this case in court. Plaintiff may seek fees/sanctions pursuant to CCP 1281.98(c) and CCP 1281.99 by a separate regularly noticed motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. ; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
